page_content: HACS497 Cybersecurity Experience Reflection (3 Credits) Cybersecurity experience is deﬁned as an experiential learning activity either with a University of Maryland entity or with an external organization that will provide valuable, hands-on experience to supplement the knowledge learned in other ACES coursework. This course is intended to help students reflect on their cybersecurity experience and to learn from others' cybersecurity experiences. It is also intended to help students gain professional skills that will aid in their future career. Prerequisite: Students may enroll concurrently with or after completing a cybersecurity related internship experience of at least 135 hours. Restriction: Must be a student in the ACES (Advanced Cybersecurity Experience for Students) Minor Program; and must not have taken HACS297. Credit Only Granted for: HACS297 or HACS497.
